plan.title: vBRIEF integration: structured plans, programmatic beads, DAG visualization
plan.author: opus-plan

narratives.Problem: The planning pipeline produces freeform PRDs and LLM-generated bd create calls. This wastes tokens, allows plan-to-bead drift, and provides no dependency graph for scheduling or visualization.
narratives.Proposal: Wire the existing createBeadsFromVBrief() converter into Cloister's post-planning step so beads creation is mechanical. Build a ReactFlow-based DAG visualization in the BeadsTasks panel. Add dependency-aware scheduling to Cloister with hard gates on blocking edges and auto-wake when tasks unblock.
narratives.Constraint: Must gracefully degrade when no plan.vbrief.json exists (legacy workspaces). Must not use execSync in any server-reachable code path (PAN-70). ReactFlow is acceptable as a new frontend dependency.
narratives.Risk: Status sync race conditions when multiple agents close beads simultaneously — mitigate with atomic read-modify-write. Cloister complexity increase — isolate in dedicated task-readiness module. Plan file absence in legacy flows — all code must handle gracefully.
narratives.Alternative: Considered dagre + custom SVG (lighter but more work for interactivity — rejected in favor of ReactFlow which matches vBRIEF Studio). Considered triggering conversion in agent prompt vs post-planning — chose post-planning in Cloister for clean separation.

items.title: Wire createBeadsFromVBrief() into Cloister post-planning step

narrative.Action: In Cloister's post-planning completion handler (after planning agent writes plan.vbrief.json and signals done), call createBeadsFromVBrief() to programmatically create beads with dependencies. This replaces LLM-generated bd create shell commands. Modify specialists.ts or the planning completion flow to detect plan.vbrief.json and trigger conversion before waking the work agent. Must handle the case where no vBRIEF plan exists (fall back to existing flow).

subItems.title: Beads are created programmatically from plan.vbrief.json after planning completes
Bead dependencies match vBRIEF blocking edges exactly
Legacy workspaces without plan.vbrief.json still work (graceful fallback)

items.title: Remove bd create instructions from planning agent prompts

narrative.Action: Update planning agent prompts in planning-agent.ts and decomposition-agent.ts to remove instructions for LLM-generated bd create calls. The planning agent should only produce plan.vbrief.json — beads creation is now handled mechanically by Cloister. May bypass decomposition-agent entirely if it only existed for bd create generation.

subItems.title: Planning agent no longer generates bd create shell commands
Zero LLM tokens spent on beads task creation

items.title: API endpoint to serve vBRIEF plan data

narrative.Action: Add GET /api/workspaces/:issueId/plan endpoint to dashboard server. Reads plan.vbrief.json from workspace .planning/ directory using vbrief/io.ts findPlan() and readPlan(). Returns the full VBriefDocument as JSON. Returns 404 if no plan exists. For remote workspaces, fetch via Fly provider.

subItems.title: GET /api/workspaces/:issueId/plan returns VBriefDocument JSON
Returns 404 when no plan.vbrief.json exists

items.title: Add ReactFlow dependency and base DAG component

narrative.Action: Install reactflow in the frontend package. Create a base PlanDAG component that accepts a VBriefDocument and renders nodes/edges. Set up custom node types for plan items with status-colored borders. Set up edge types: solid for blocks, dashed for informs. Use dagre or elkjs for automatic layout (top-to-bottom).

subItems.title: reactflow is installed in frontend/package.json
Base PlanDAG component renders a simple test graph

items.title: Build DAG graph with status colors, edge styling, and layout

narrative.Action: Build the full DAG visualization component. Map vBRIEF items to ReactFlow nodes with: status colors (gray=pending, blue=running, green=completed, red=blocked, yellow=cancelled), difficulty badge, priority indicator. Map edges with visual distinction: solid=blocks, dashed=informs, dotted=suggests. Auto-layout with dagre (top-to-bottom flow). Support zoom, pan, minimap. Fetch plan data from GET /api/workspaces/:issueId/plan.

subItems.title: Nodes show status color, title, difficulty, and priority
Edge types are visually distinct (solid=blocks, dashed=informs)
Graph auto-layouts with dagre (no manual positioning needed)

items.title: Augment BeadsTasks panel with graph/list toggle

narrative.Action: Add a toggle button (list icon / graph icon) to BeadsTasksPanel header. When in list mode, show existing beads list view. When in graph mode, show the PlanDAG component. Default to list view. Only show toggle when plan.vbrief.json exists for the workspace. Persist toggle preference in localStorage.

subItems.title: Toggle switches between list view and DAG graph view
Toggle only appears when workspace has a vBRIEF plan

items.title: Click-through from graph node to item/bead detail

narrative.Action: When clicking a node in the DAG graph, show a detail panel/popover with: item title, narrative, acceptance criteria (with pass/fail status from subItems), difficulty, priority, corresponding bead status. Link to bead detail if the bead exists. Show blockers and dependents from the edge data.

subItems.title: Clicking a node shows item narrative, acceptance criteria, and bead status
Detail shows blockers and dependent items

narrative.Action: When a bead is closed (agent completes task), update the corresponding vBRIEF item status in plan.vbrief.json. Add updateItemStatus() to vbrief/io.ts that does atomic read-modify-write. Hook into bead completion events (likely in specialists.ts or the bead completion handler). Match beads to vBRIEF items by title pattern ('{issueId}: {item.title}'). Handle race conditions with file locking or retry.

subItems.title: Closing a bead updates the vBRIEF item status to 'completed'
Race conditions handled (multiple beads closing simultaneously)

items.title: Live DAG updates via socket.io as beads complete

narrative.Action: Emit socket.io events when vBRIEF item status changes (after status-sync writes). Frontend subscribes and updates DAG node colors in real-time without polling. Use existing socket.io infrastructure. Event shape: { issueId, itemId, newStatus }. Frontend React Query invalidation or direct Zustand update.

subItems.title: DAG node colors update in real-time when beads complete
No polling required — updates pushed via socket.io

items.title: Dependency-aware task readiness check in Cloister

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/cloister/task-readiness.ts module. Implement isTaskReady(taskId, workspacePath) that: reads plan.vbrief.json, gets blocking edges for the task, checks if all blockers have status 'completed' or 'cancelled'. Integrate into wakeSpecialistOrQueue() in specialists.ts — before scheduling a task, verify its dependencies are met. If not ready, queue with 'blocked' reason. Gracefully handle workspaces without vBRIEF plans (all tasks are ready by default).

subItems.title: Tasks with incomplete blocking dependencies are NOT scheduled
Workspaces without plan.vbrief.json work normally (no regression)

items.title: Auto-detect unblocked tasks and wake specialists

narrative.Action: When a bead completes (and status-sync updates vBRIEF), check getUnblockedItems() for newly unblocked tasks. For each newly unblocked task, wake the appropriate specialist via wakeSpecialistOrQueue(). This creates an event-driven DAG execution: complete task A → check what A was blocking → wake tasks whose blockers are all done. Integrate with existing specialist queue system. Prevent duplicate wake-ups.

subItems.title: Completing a bead automatically wakes specialists for newly unblocked downstream tasks
No duplicate wake-ups when multiple blockers complete simultaneously

items.title: Critical path algorithm and dashboard highlight

narrative.Action: Implement criticalPath(doc: VBriefDocument): string[] in the vBRIEF DAG utilities. Uses longest-path algorithm on blocking edges (all edges have weight 1 unless difficulty metadata provides estimates). Returns ordered list of item IDs on the critical path. In the DAG visualization, highlight critical path nodes and edges with a distinct color (e.g., orange border, thicker edges). Show critical path length in the plan panel header.

subItems.title: Critical path algorithm returns the longest dependency chain
Critical path nodes and edges are visually highlighted in the DAG
